summary: False-positive resistance: the prompt contains zero subjective terms — every adjective and verb is concrete and operationally defined. The LLM MUST NOT call request_interpretation_review at all. If it does, the surfacing heuristic is over-eager and will drown real subjective surfaces in noise.
opening_prompt: load the CSV file at /data/orders.csv, filter rows where the amount column is greater than 100, and save the filtered rows to /out/orders_filtered.json

phase5b_assertions.must_not_call_request_interpretation_rev: Zero subjective terms. 'greater than 100' is fully specified. 'filter' is a concrete operation. 'CSV', 'JSON', '/data/orders.csv' are all concrete. Inspect chat_messages.tool_calls and confirm tool_name='request_interpretation_review' is ABSENT for the entire session.
phase5b_assertions.must_not_emit_any_interpretation_placeho: Inspect state.nodes[*].options.prompt_template and confirm no {{interpretation:...}} placeholder appears anywhere in the staged pipeline. There is no LLM transform in this pipeline, so this should be trivially satisfied — but a model that hallucinates an LLM step would also fail this check.

build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
